title: slash_login uses tokio::runtime::Handle::current() unwrap-style — panics outside a runtime
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/cli/repl/slash.rs:569-587, src/cli/repl/slash.rs:443-456
## Violation
slash_login calls Handle::current() without try_current() inside a synchronous slash handler. Quoted lines:

    if let Ok(creds) = tokio::runtime::Handle::current().block_on(openclaudia::claude_credentials::load_credentials()) {
        println!("  Type: {}", creds.subscription_type.as_deref().unwrap_or("unknown"));
        ...
    }

`Handle::current()` panics when called outside a runtime — and the slash dispatcher does not guarantee one is present (the function lives in a sync REPL handler). slash_model at line 446-455 correctly uses `Handle::try_current()` then if-lets — the asymmetry inside the same file shows the author knew the safer pattern but did not apply it uniformly. Calling `Handle::current().block_on(...)` from inside an async runtime ALSO panics (cannot block_on the current runtime from a worker thread).
## Mandated refactor
Replace with `if let Ok(handle) = tokio::runtime::Handle::try_current() { handle.block_on(...) }` matching slash_model's pattern. Better: make the whole slash dispatcher async (it already returns through into a tokio context) so block_on disappears entirely and the credentials fetch awaits naturally.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
